translated: The man who has no wife is called a widower; the woman who has no husband is called a widow.

ref: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
text: N tan sa jii pei ki semm n nuudiitu po, ŋaan jii buunii na mun ki semm n nuugaŋ po.
translated: If you go to the farm and leave your mother at home, you will go to the bush and leave your father at home.

ref: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
text: Ŋaan wa din poi binba maŋ din ki gbiint ki tur u daaba, ŋaan yaat yaat ki saan bi toona ni: ki yenɔ doo u kpaab ni, ki lɔɔ mun saan u bont kɔiu daak ni.
translated: If a child does not listen to the words of his father and mother, and keeps on doing what he wants: he will go to the bush with them, and they will leave him there and return home alone.

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Li paak, ki bi kar dukin likirii maŋ po, ki tan jii saan saa daa yɔkmarɔɔ tiŋ a bii pii saamm leŋ.
translated: The man, who was walking along the river bank, saw a crocodile coming out of the water and quickly ran away.

ref: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
text: Bi jint niib jikikpiata linba paar nan bin jii, ŋaan kan sak ki bin jii bi niibir gbaa ki babir sɔɔ ki wun jii.
translated: When a child is born, the family does not rejoice immediately; they wait to see if the child will live before they celebrate.

ref: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
text: Yisa din yiin barjokite ki piak linba na kura ki teen nibur maŋ. U din ki pak siar ki turib ki li ki tee barjokit ni kaa.
translated: One day a hunter went to the bush and killed a lion and brought it home. He gave the meat to his wives and they cooked it and ate it with the hunter.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: “Binba mɔk ninbatinu nan bi leeb, parpeenn tee bi yare, kimaan ŋamme ki Yennu mun bia sa timm ninbaauk.
translated: The Binba people say that when a child is born, if it cries, it means that Yennu has allowed the child to stay in this world.

ref: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
text: Piita nba kar Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ ŋaadundɔuŋ ni na, ki ŋaak maŋ ni bonposiak baar u boor ni ki tan betɔ a “A mun tuu yɔɔ be nan Yisa wunba nyii Galilee na.”
translated: They brought him to the place called Golgotha (which means “the place of a skull”), and they offered him wine mixed with myrrh, but he did not take it.

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Bee ki i ki bann nan li ki tee boroboro po kaa ki n piaki? I mi man Farisiinba nan Sajusiinba datinii na po.”
translated: Who is the one who came to destroy the one who was already destroyed? I am the one called Farisiinba and Sajusiinba in this place.

ref: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: N ji sa wanni nan min nisaarik Bik mɔk yikoo tingbouŋ na ni ki sa nyik chab niib bi biit;” ŋaan jiant bet jɔɔ nba ki u gbanant kpo na a “Fiit ki jii a liaŋ kii kun a ŋaak ni.”
translated: I went to someone and told him that Bik had said that Tingbouŋ had said that he would come and eat the food of the children; then he replied, “If that is so, let him come and we shall see.”
